page: Vultr vcg-a40-8c-40g-16vram is a virtual machine instance within the Cloud GPU family. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel processor with 8 shared vCPUs and 8 physical cores, operating at 1.0 thread per core. The system is configured with 40.0 GB of system memory, providing a ratio of 5.0 GB of RAM per core.

This instance integrates a fractional NVIDIA Ampere A40 GPU, representing approximately one-third of a physical GPU, with 16 GB of dedicated VRAM. Local storage is provided via a 740 GB NVMe SSD. The network configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

The fractional GPU allocation and shared CPU model offer a cost-efficient entry point for workloads requiring GPU acceleration without the expense of a dedicated full GPU. However, the shared CPU allocation introduces potential resource contention, which may affect workloads requiring sustained, predictable CPU performance.

This server configuration is suitable for workloads such as small-scale machine learning inference, model development, data analysis, and moderate graphics rendering tasks that benefit from GPU acceleration but do not require a full dedicated GPU or constant maximum CPU throughput.
description: Vultr vcg-a40-8c-40g-16vram is a Cloud GPU instance designed for workloads requiring moderate GPU acceleration. It features an Intel x86_64 processor with 8 shared vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and 40.0 GB of system memory. The instance is equipped with a fractional NVIDIA Ampere A40 GPU, providing 16 GB of dedicated VRAM, alongside 740 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. By utilizing a shared CPU model and a fractional GPU allocation of approximately one-third of a physical card, this instance offers a cost-efficient alternative to dedicated GPU hardware. This resource profile is suitable for small-scale machine learning inference, model development, data analysis, and moderate graphics rendering tasks where a full dedicated GPU is not required.
